items.title: Family thanks community as tributes flow in for Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: The family of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs on Thursday, has thanked the community for their support as tributes flow in from across the country.

articles.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family calls for calm following Alice riots

children.content: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

image.altText: A man stands inside a house.

articles.title: Alice Springs erupts in violence after 5yo girl's alleged killer arrested

children.content: Wild scenes erupted on the streets of Alice Springs last night following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis over the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A person picking up a smoke canister on the street at night with crowds in the background.

articles.title: Jefferson Lewis arrested over alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Jefferson Lewis has been the subject of a police manhunt, and is accused of murdering the young girl in Alice Springs.

image.altText: Flowers on a fence

gallery.altText: A teddy bear among floral tributes

thumbnail.altText: A teddy bear among floral tributes

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

image.altText: A drone shot of saltbush and roads in Alice Springs

items.title: Family of Tiwi Islands sistergirl calls for life sentence for man who killed her

children.content: The family of a beloved Tiwi Islands sistergirl has pleaded for the man who killed her to be handed a life sentence, describing more than a year of sleepless nights, heartbreak and grief.

image.altText: A family stands in front of the NT Supreme Court looking sad and wearing Justice for Lena shirts.

items.title: Chinese company launches legal action over forced sale of Port of Darwin

children.content: Anthony Albanese pledged at the federal election that Labor would return the port to Australian hands, saying it necessary for national security.

gallery.altText: A large piece of equipment on the port.

thumbnail.altText: A large piece of equipment on the port.

items.title: Tough-on-crime NT government snubs youth incarceration Senate inquiry

children.content: The inquiry heard calls for more scrutiny over how Commonwealth money is spent in the NT, which is trending backwards on seven Closing the Gap targets.

image.altText: A group of young Indigenous children are playing basketball inside the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre.

items.title: NT paramedics 'beaten' to the point of 'semi-consciousness'

children.content: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

image.altText: An ambulance pulled side of road at dusk in city

items.title: NT government accused of hiding long-term watch house detentions

children.content: Just weeks after the CLP government insisted the Northern Territory's watch house crisis was over, the NT's Aboriginal justice agency has accused it of renaming parts of a Darwin watch house to conceal that people are still being held there long term.

image.altText: Low-angle shot of Darwin city watch house building, with blue and white checkered tiles on front of building in view.

items.title: Road safety advocates want tighter import standards for big utes

children.content: Sales of large utes have increased by 270 per cent since 2019, data from a national transport safety body.

image.altText: A big black ute parked with two wheels on the kerb

items.title: Polls close in Nepean by-election in Victoria

children.content: The Liberal Party seeks to retain the seat vacated by former Victorian deputy Liberal leader Sam Groth.

image.altText: A composite image of a man with a cap saying "One Nation", a man in a navy cap and a woman in pink cap, all smiling.

items.title: Israeli police video shows attack on nun in Jerusalem

children.content: Israeli police release a video showing what eyewitnesses say was an attack by a Jewish man on a nun near Jerusalem's Old City. Police say the suspect, a 36-year-old man, was later arrested.

image.altText: A nun is lying on the ground, in a walled street, as a man stands over her. Two bystanders are walking towards the scene.

items.title: US to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany amid Trump spat with Merz

children.content: The United States is withdrawing 5,000 troops from NATO ally Germany amid a growing a rift between the nations' leaders over the Iran war.

image.altText: A man with dark suit, white shirt and red tie, smiles. He's standing to the left of the picture against a dark black background.

items.title: Iran's nuclear deal was fragile and it didn't survive Trump

children.content: A secret 2013 meeting inside New York’s Waldorf Astoria helped produce a landmark deal to curb Iran’s nuclear program.
After Donald Trump tore it up, escalation replaced diplomacy — leaving the world once again wondering whether any deal with Iran can truly last.

items.title: Second of two new Spirit of Tasmania ferries arrives in Geelong

children.content: Following a seven-week journey from Scotland, the Spirit of Tasmania V has arrived in Geelong. It will undergo gantry testing tomorrow before being temporarily relocated to Station Pier in Melbourne.

image.altText: A large red and white ferry with SPIRIT OF TASMANIA on it sailing through a bay.

items.title: Why this cattle farmer is flooding his property with salt water

children.content: More than half a century ago, lush wetlands along the Queensland coastline were cut off from the ocean as farmers built physical barriers to stop salt water flowing into their land. Now, conservation groups and traditional owners are restoring tidal flows and bringing life back to the land.

image.altText: A man stands with a large branch over his shoulders, looking into the distance.

items.title: How young actor's big break ended in court battle with Hollywood star

children.content: Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson are locked in a messy defamation battle involving disparaging social media posts, a "Russian oligarch" and sexual harassment accusations.

items.title: Sabotage and violence alleged on major Brisbane rail project

items.title: 1976 in music: The big albums turning 50 this year

children.content: The music industry is always in a state of flux, but what was happening in 1976 was akin to the dinosaurs looking up and seeing a meteor on the way.

items.title: This pumpkin soup is comfort food with a twist that takes it to the next level

children.content: Pumpkin soup is one of a few meals with just one main ingredient. This delicious version spices things up with coconut cream and an easy curry-leaf oil.

image.altText: A bowl of blended orange soup. On top are two leaves, dots of oil, a dollop of coconut cream and some black pepper.

items.title: The four women who left 'Australia Street' to try and leave Syria

children.content: Four Australian women, their children and grandchildren are stuck in limbo in Syria after leaving a refugee camp for family members of suspected Islamic State fighters. This is what we know about them.

image.altText: A group of women wearing hijabs and face masks, accompanied by a group of children.

items.title: Gas vs beer: How Pocock caused the government a tax headache

children.content: David Pocock was struggling to find cut-through on the issue of gas taxes, but then a question about the beer excise sparked a social media war of words.

articles.title: Albanese says US 'playing a different role' in world

children.content: Anthony Albanese says the United States is "playing a different role" in the world under President Donald Trump, forcing Australia and allies to adjust how they respond to unexpected conflicts.

articles.title: Tie property tax changes to income tax cuts, productivity chief says

children.content: Productivity Commission chair Danielle Wood says there is a "case" for property tax changes, but for it to be serious reform, the economic benefit must flow to workers.

items.title: Shift in the Middle East has implications for countries like Australia

children.content: Donald Trump's earlier signalling that he will neither walk away from the war declaring a victory or escalate it, but will try to starve the Iranians out, suggests there is no clear sign of how this war could end.

image.altText: An older man from the UAE in a white robe and head covering looks slightly concerned. The background is blue.

items.title: Iran's race: Break the US blockade or risk its economic lifeline

children.content: Analysis of how much oil Iran is producing, shipping and having to store shows pressure is mounting on Tehran, but while experts agree the US blockade is effective, there are questions about whether it can really force Iran back to the negotiating table.

image.altText: Ships and boats in the Strait of Hormuz, with the sun setting behind hills in the back.

items.title: Cosmetics will soon cost more. Here's why

items.title: Fiji considers state of emergency after suspected drug kingpin murder in custody

children.content: The suspected murder of a Fijian underworld figure held in military custody has sparked a new chapter in the island nation's wrestle with drug-related crimes, with the police and military combining forces for a highly visible "crackdown".
